skillName: Use and interpret inequalities in context (incl number lines and statements)
misconceptionSummary: Students misapply inequality symbols when negative values are involved.

visuals.ruleText: For negatives: closer to zero = greater value
visuals.subText: -2 > -6  because -2 is only 2 steps from 0, while -6 is 6 steps away

steps.narration: Think of negative numbers as debts. Owing two pounds is better than owing six. Less debt — closer to zero — means a greater value.
The arrow points right — towards the greater value. Negative two is to the right of negative six, so negative two is greater. The line makes this visual and unambiguous.
Negative four is closer to zero, so it is greater. The open end of the symbol faces it. Greater than.

visuals.label: Closer to zero wins for negative numbers

steps.narration: Closer to zero, further right on the line, greater value. Those three ideas all say the same thing.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: using the symbol based on digit size alone. For negatives, a larger digit means further from zero — which means smaller.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Negative numbers work the opposite way to what you might expect. More negative means smaller, not larger.
